rulings.Case Title: C. OVERAA & CO., INC. VS. U.S. GLASS & ALUMINUM, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Re-Open Discovery On A Limited Basis
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 13, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF C. OVERAA & CO.'s Motion To Re-Open Discovery On A Limited Basis. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative; parties to appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@gbgllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANITA COEN VS. GENERAL NUTRITION CENTERS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Obtain An Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff Anita Coen
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT GENERAL NUTRITION CENTERS, INC., DARIN VILHAUER Motion To Obtain An Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff Anita Coen. Defendants' motion for an IME of plaintiff is DENIED. The discovery cutoff in this case passed 10 days ago, April 3. Defendants concede they knew plaintiff was claiming emotional distress 20 months ago, but failed to diligently seek an IME. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: NISSIM NINIO VS. WILLIAM AYOUBI, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2015, Line 1, MARCELA PACALDO Motion For Appointment Of Expert. The motion for a court-appointed business-valuation expert under Evidence Code sec. 730 is DENIED. Discovery can yield the information the parties' own valuation experts would need - any recalcitrance can be addressed by a motion to compel. Moreover, "safeguarding" of assets is not the kind of activity a business-valuation expert would typically perform. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL S. LOZIER et al VS. CAHILL CONTRACTORS INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT CAHILL CONTRACTORS INC., MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff Lozier was an employee of a fire-sprinkler subcontractor of general contractor Cahill who slipped and fell on a ramp at Cahill's jobsite. There are triable issues of material fact as to whether Cahill (1) retained control of the ramp where the accident took place; and (2) negligently exercised that control in a manner that affirmatively contributed to Plaintiff's injury. See, e.g., pp. 6-10 of Plaintiffs' Opposition to Cahill's Separate Statement of Material Facts; see also McKown, 27 Cal. 4th 219; CACI 1009D. Cahill's motion for summary judgment is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CRYSTAL LEI ET AL VS. DEMAS W YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration, Sever Causes Of Action C And D And Stay Action As To Defendant Allen Matkins Only
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT ALLEN MATKINS LECK GAMBLE MALLORY & NATSIS LLP Motion To Compel Arbitration, Sever Causes Of Action C And D And Stay Action As To Defendant Allen Matkins Only. Allen Matkins' motion to compel arbitration, sever causes of action and stay is GRANTED. The retention agreement makes arbitrable "any additional matters we handle on your behalf." Bryant Fu is also bound as he received the benefit of the retention agreement and it was signed pursuant to the duties and rights of his guardian. rulings.Case Title: CRYSTAL LEI ET AL VS. DEMAS W YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Declare Demas Yan To Be A Vexatious Litigant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CRYSTAL LEI, BRYANT FU Motion To Declare Demas Yan To Be A Vexatious Litigant. Plaintiff Lei's motion to declare defendant Yan a vexatious litigant and to impose a prefiling order is GRANTED. However, the motion to require Demas to furnish security for any motion is DENIED, because CCP sec. 391.1 applies only to a "plaintiff" and Yan is a defendant in this case. The cases Plaintiff Lei cites are not to the contrary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC VS. 215-1 - 215-2 -217 COLE STREET HOMEOWNERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT 215-1 - 215-2 - 217 COLE STREET HOMEOWNERS Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Arbitration. Defendants' motion to compel arbitration is DENIED. As shown in pages 7-10 of Plaintiff's brief, "there is a possibility of conflicting rulings on issues of law or fact." CCP sec. 1281.2(c). This is particularly so as the Contractors would not participate in the arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FLIGHTEN UP, LLC ET AL VS. ZOOMSYSTEMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendants Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Cross-Complainant Gower Smiths Entire Complaint And Each And Every Cause Of Action Thereof Under Ccp Section 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2015, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT FLIGHTEN UP, LLC, CHRIS MICHAILOV, RYAN BORLAND Cross Defendants Special Motion To Strike Cross-Complainant Gower Smiths Entire Complaint And Each And Every Cause Of Action Thereof Under CCP Section 425.16. Plaintiffs filed an anti-SLAPP motion against G. Smith's cross-complaint. G. Smith subsequently dismissed the cross-complaint, so this Court loses jurisdiction to rule on the motion, but may decide attorneys fees and costs under CCP sec. 425.16. Smith apparently does not dispute $9,685 of fees and costs, but does dispute another $6,500. A hearing will be required. Given the limited issues and modest amount of money involved, out-of-town counsel may appear telephonically if they desire. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)
